76
81

This is good progress. 
However, I must push back against the idea that we need a named tunnel to test passkeys. 
We have already demonstrated in a previous project in golang 
https://github.com/kusl/gotunnels
that it is not only possible to add passkeys to a user in a try cloudflare dot com tunnel 
but it is possible to have a passkey ONLY flow in a try cloudflare dot com tunnel as well 
meaning all the user has is a username and a passkey 
Please review this and make necessary course corrections 
not just in the code but the documentation as well 
notice how the go tunnels is able to start the API application, the frontend application, all in a perfect symfony 
```
[2/2] STEP 1/6: FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12:nonroot
[2/2] STEP 2/6: WORKDIR /
--> Using cache e14ab4df47cf49d299ef67fca396aff23ad8f94f577a9affba7a1f3e454ca183
--> e14ab4df47cf
[2/2] STEP 3/6: COPY --from=build /out/api /api
--> 703d1694d024
[2/2] STEP 4/6: USER nonroot:nonroot
--> 6813e73d8ebb
[2/2] STEP 5/6: EXPOSE 8080
--> dfd5f4e843c9
[2/2] STEP 6/6: ENTRYPOINT ["/api"]
[2/2] COMMIT gotunnels-q6c9jg_api
--> bb68f08232c8
```

I have attached dump.txt and virginia.txt from the go tunnels project for your review 
Please continue with the next step 
and the structured tar gz was a good idea 
It removes the guess work of which file goes where 
if I can paste the whole folder together in one go 
please do that if possible 
remember to tell me which files if any I would need to delete 
I have also updated the build_progress.md file
